story_prompt: make a lecture on js class

scenes.text: In JavaScript, a class is a special type of function that serves as a blueprint for creating objects with pre-defined properties and methods, allowing developers to use object-oriented programming concepts. The class syntax introduced in ECMAScript 6 provides a more straightforward and cleaner way to create objects and handle inheritance, making code easier to write and understand.

A powerful feature of JavaScript classes is inheritance, which allows a class to inherit properties and methods from another class. This promotes code reuse and logical abstraction. By using the 'extends' keyword, a new class can inherit all the properties and behaviors of another class, and you can also override or add new methods to extend functionality further.

JavaScript classes provide the concept of static methods. Static methods are called on the class itself rather than on instances of the class. These methods are often used for utility functions related to the class that do not require an instance to operate. Static methods are declared using the 'static' keyword, offering developers tools to execute tasks essential to the class as a whole.
